title: A Switching-like Dynamic Event-Triggered Sliding Mode Control for T-S Fuzzy Singular Markov Jump Systems under Hybrid Cyber-Attacks
authors: Zhipeng Li, Shuanghe Yu, Yan Yan, Ying Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: This paper investigates the secure control problem for a class of T-S fuzzy singular Markov jump systems (SMJSs) under limited communication resources and hybrid cyber-attacks. To address the communication interruptions and hindered data updates caused by denial-of-service (DoS) attacks, a novel dual-dynamic switching-like event-triggered scheme (DDSETS) is proposed. Meanwhile, considering multiple adverse factors including external disturbances, mode-dependent deception attacks, and system parameter uncertainties, an asynchronous sliding mode controller (ASMC) is designed to mitigate these adverse effects, and the asynchronous phenomenon is characterized by the Hidden Markov Model (HMM). By constructing a Lyapunov functional, sufficient conditions for the stochastic admissibility of the closed-loop system under a prescribed <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> performance index are derived, based on which the event-triggering weighting matrices and controller gains are obtained. Furthermore, it is rigorously proven that under the proposed sliding mode control law, the predefined sliding surface can be reached in finite time. Finally, numerical simulations conducted on a mass-spring-damper system demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed scheme compared to existing methods.

title: Homotopy-Guided Potential Games for Congestion-Aware Navigation_supp4-3675907.mp4
authors: Mohammed Irshadh Ismaaeel Sathyamangalam Imran
publish: N/A
abstract: We address the multi-agent motion planning problem where interactions, collisions, and congestion co-exist. Conventional game-theoretic planners capture interactions among agents but often converge to conservative, congested equilibria. Homotopy planners, on the other hand, can explore topologically distinct paths, but lack mechanisms to account for the interdependence of agents' future actions. We propose a unified framework that leverages homotopy classes as structured strategy sets within a receding-horizon setup. At each planning stage, a deterministic homotopy planner generates topologically distinct paths for each agent, conditioned on the joint configuration. To avoid intractable growth of candidate paths, we propose a simple heuristic filtering step that selects a top-K subset of the most suitable congestion-free joint strategies to ensure computational tractability. These serve as initializations for a potential game that enforces homotopy-consistent constraints and yields a generalized open-loop Nash equilibrium (OLNE), with penalties discouraging abrupt strategy shifts in a receding-horizon setting. Simulations with three agents demonstrate improved efficiency (faster completion) and enhanced safety (greater inter-agent clearance, leading to reduced congestion) compared to a local baseline and NH ORCA that do not reason about homotopies. Hardware trials with two robots and one human demonstrate robustness to irrational behaviors, where our method adapts by switching to alternative feasible equilibria while the baseline game fails.

title: Cosmos-Surg-dVRK: World Foundation Model-based Automated Online Evaluation of Surgical Robot Policy Learning_supp1-3675962.mp4
authors: Lukas Zbinden
publish: N/A
abstract: The rise of robot-assisted surgery and vision language-action models has accelerated progress in autonomous surgical policies and efficient assessment strategies. However, evaluating these policies directly on physical robotic platforms such as the da Vinci Research Kit (dVRK) remains hindered by high costs, time demands, reproducibility challenges, and variability in execution. World foundation models (WFM) for physical AI offer a transformative approach to simulate complex real-world surgical tasks, such as soft tissue deformation, with a high degree of realism. This work introduces Cosmos-Surg dVRK, a surgical finetune of the Cosmos WFM, which, together with a trained video classifier, enables fully automated online evaluation and benchmarking of surgical policies. We evaluate Cosmos-Surg-dVRK using two independent, teleoperated, real world dVRK surgical datasets. On tabletop suture pad tasks, the automated pipeline achieves strong correlation between online rollouts in Cosmos-Surg-dVRK and policy outcomes on the real dVRK Si platform, as well as good agreement between human labelers and the V-JEPA 2-derived video classifier. Additionally, preliminary experiments using the state-of-the-art SRT-H surgical policy for ex-vivo porcine cholecystectomy tasks demonstrate promising alignment between Cosmos-Surg-dVRK and real-world dVRK evaluations, highlighting the platform's potential for more complex surgical procedures.

title: A Framework for Predictive Similarity Queries Over Heterogeneous Metric Spaces
authors: Thiago G. Vespa, Igor A. R. Eleutério, Agma J. M. Traina, Fabio M. Bertato, Caetano Traina
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: In this research, we introduce the concept of predictive multimodal queries within the framework of multi-metric spaces, establishing a foundation for predictive analysis through similarity-based retrieval. We present a reverse regression algorithm specifically designed to exploit the expressiveness of multi-metric queries, enabling the merging and comparative analysis of results across distinct metric spaces. Two fundamental operations are formalized: the multi-metric intersection and union queries, which serve as the foundation for predictive analysis. Building upon these, we define three advanced types of multi-metric queries: the Union-Representative Query (URQ), the Multi-Representative Query (MRQ), and the Headed-Representative Query (HRQ), each supporting a distinct predictive strategy. These methods bridge the gap between similarity-based retrieval and attribute prediction, extending the analytical capability of metric-space models toward multimodal predictive analysis. To ensure practical applicability, we developed a computational framework, including both an engine and an open-source library, providing tools for the implementation of multi-metric queries and reverse regression techniques.

title: A Spatio-Temporal Hierarchical Diffusion Framework for Training-Free Perceptual Remote Sensing Image Compression_supp1-3675642.pdf
authors: Liang Yongsheng
publish: N/A
abstract: Remote sensing data contains vast amounts of geographic structural information and fine-grained textural details, making compression essential and necessitating efficient processing for downstream interpretation tasks. Generative models have shown great potential in learned image compression, offering high compression rates and exceptional generation quality. Among these, diffusion models stand out for their ability to produce high-quality reconstructions. However, they may hallucinate small structures and textures due to significant information loss at limited bitrates. Furthermore, compression based on the diffusion process suffers from long inference times caused by numerous denoising steps, which hinders practical deployment. We propose Spatio-Temporal Hierarchical Codebook Diffusion (STHCD), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates resources to align with this hierarchy. STHCD introduces three synergistic components: (1) Spatial Residual Transformation via parameter-free unshuffle operations to adapt spatial granularity; (2) Channel-wise Codebook Splitting for flexible bit allocation without memory explosion; and (3) a Time-Dependent Hierarchical Policy to orchestrate these operations across timesteps. Extensive experiments on nine datasets show that STHCD significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in perceptual metrics (LPIPS, DISTS, FID). At equivalent bitrates (∼0.12 bpp), STHCD achieves an 8∼12.5× speedup (80∼125 vs. 1000 steps) over standard temporal codebook diffusion while optimizing LPIPS by 29.5%. Furthermore, STHCD can achieve a remarkable 15-step diffusion at the same bitrate, with only a minor LPIPS drop, establishing a new paradigm for hierarchical remote sensing compression. Our project is available at https://sthcd.github.io.

title: CNNGP: Multi-tree Genetic Programming Integrating Pre-trained CNN Features for Fine-Grained Image Classification_supp1-3675672.pdf
authors: Ying Bi
publish: N/A
abstract: Fine-grained image classification (FGIC) remains a challenging task due to subtle inter-class differences and significant intra-class variations, particularly under limited training data. While genetic programming (GP) offers promising adaptability, the performance of existing GP-based methods is limited due to the reliance on traditional image descriptors for feature extraction/learning. To address this limitation, this paper proposes CNNGP, i.e., a novel multi-tree GP approach that utilizes the rich representations of pre-trained convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for FGIC. In CNNGP’s multi-tree representation, two trees are used to explore multi-level feature maps from pre-trained CNNs to generate saliency masks that highlight informative regions, and one tree is used to integrate them to construct higher-level and more powerful features for classification. Experiments on four commonly used FGIC datasets demonstrate that CNNGP can achieve superior (or at least competitive) performance to popular deep neural network methods and the recent GP-based method. Further analysis verifies the benefit of using dual saliency trees and visualizes how CNNGP captures complementary visual patterns that enhance feature learning. It also examines convolutional layer selection to reveal dataset-specific feature usage, demonstrating CNNGP’s adaptive feature learning capability.

title: Multi-Scale Fully-Connected Tensor Network Decomposition with NSST Guidance for Thick Cloud Removal
authors: Ming-Qing Li, Yu-Qing Wang, Hui-Lan Lin, Meng-Yang Pan, Yang Hang, De-Hao Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Thick cloud coverage presents a significant challenge for the analysis and application of multi-temporal remote sensing imagery. Although existing cloud removal methods attempt to exploit spatio-spectral-temporal correlations, they often fail to adequately capture cross-scale complementary features due to single-scale reconstruction limitations, resulting in texture degradation and color distortion effects. To address this issue, we propose a multi-scale fully connected tensor network decomposition method guided by the non-subsampled shearlet transform (NSST). The proposed approach first applies NSST in the spatial domain, followed by parameter-adaptive pulse-coupled neural network (PCNN) fusion of each decomposed subband along the temporal dimension to construct a high-quality guidance image. This guidance image is subsequently employed to impose regularization constraints on both high- and low-frequency components of the tensor network decomposition in the NSST domain, enabling a comprehensive representation of multi-scale correlations. To efficiently solve the resulting optimization model, we develop a proximal alternating minimization (PAM)-based algorithm. Extensive experiments on both simulated and real satellite datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms existing approaches, achieving average improvements of 1.2–3.5 dB in PSNR and 0.5–2.0% in SSIM over the best baseline across various cloud coverage scenarios, while maintaining superior detail preservation and color consistency.

title: Diagnosis of Open-Circuit Faults in Single-Phase Three-Level Rectifiers Under Unknown Parameter Using VDFRLS and Discrete Adaptive Sliding Mode Observer
authors: Shuiqing Xu, Yiming Zheng, Jian Sun, Youqiang Hu, Xi Yang, Yi Chai, Wei Xing Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To address the problem of open-circuit (OC) fault diagnosis in power switch of single-phase three-level (SPTL) rectifiers under unknown parameter conditions, this paper proposes a diagnostic method that integrates the variable direction forgetting recursive least squares (VDFRLS) algorithm with the discrete adaptive sliding mode observer (DASMO). Firstly, a VDFRLS algorithm is proposed, which addresses the problem in traditional least squares methods where high correlation between voltage and current data may cause the information matrix to become nearly singular and lead to abnormal parameter updates, thereby obtaining more accurate rectifier’s impedance parameters. Subsequently, a DASMO is designed, which leverages its rapid convergence and reduced chattering to achieve precise system state estimation. An adaptive threshold for fault detection is then developed using the residual current on the grid-side and its norm, ensuring both the accuracy and robustness of the detection algorithm. Furthermore, an innovative fault localization mechanism combining half-wave current sliding window integration with an observer under fault conditions is proposed, enabling precise localization of OC faults in power switch. Finally, hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) experiments validate that the proposed method is both effective and robust.

title: Pulse Compression for Long Spread Spectrum Signals Application in Air-Coupled Ultrasonic Thickness Resonance Spectroscopy
authors: Linas Svilainis, Muhammad Tayyib
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Interfering reflections occur due to air impedance mismatch from sample and transducer in non-contact resonance ultrasound spectroscopy (NC-RUS) measurements. The transducer is in close vicinity to the sample in order to ensure low attenuation in the air and flat wavefront. Therefore, interfering reflections are at 30-100 μs distance from the useful signal. Pulse excitation is used to avoid the overlap that can distort the measurement. A short pulse is required to ensure a broadband excitation, but such a pulse is low energy. Air-to-sample impedance mismatch further reduces signal energy and SNR. Therefore, very high voltage excitation and averaging is required. High voltage excitation increases nonlinearity effect in air, averaging increases measurement time. Long spread spectrum (SS) signals can achieve high SNR, avoid distortion in air, and expand the bandwidth available for analysis. However, long SS signals will overlap. Here it was demonstrated that the pulse compression (PuC) using matched filter is not suitable: the correlation sidelobes overlap with interfering reflections and bias errors of NC-RUS measurements increase. Solution proposed for PuC uses inverse filter. Such compressed signal corresponds to ideal pulse excitation (uniform spectrum) and has no sidelobes. If the spectral content of the SS needs to be preserved, then the PuC result can be gated and decompressed back. It was demonstrated that errors can be significantly reduced in such a case. For example, 0.008% sample density estimation bias error is achieved in case of 350 μs long excitation compared to 2.42% when using matched filter or 2.1% in case of mismatched filter, a 300 times reduction.

title: Robust and Scalable Deep Learning Architecture for NFC-Based Physical Layer Security in Dynamic Wireless Environments
authors: Sritulasi Adigopula, MV Subramanyam
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: With the growing adoption of NFC in finance, healthcare, and access control, ensuring physical layer security is critical. Relay attacks and spoofing exploit noise, channel distortion, and hardware diversity, where traditional cryptographic and classical ML methods fall short. While deep learning can learn RF fingerprints, challenges remain in cross-reader generalization, low-SNR robustness, and edge deployment. To address these, we propose NFCScaleAI, powered by the ScaleSecureNet model. It integrates multi-view signal fusion (I/Q, polar, spectrogram), domain-adversarial training, and triplet-based embedding optimization for robustness and scalability. A lightweight Mobile1DNet student model, enhanced with quantization-aware training and early-exit mechanisms, enables real-time deployment on Raspberry Pi and Android NFC. Experiments on NFC_Relay show 97.6% accuracy, 96.3% macro-F1, and 1.9% EER, outperforming baselines and the earlier IQSecureNet. Scalability tests confirmed stable detection under new devices and tags, while Grad-CAM, saliency heatmaps, and prototype retrieval validated explainability. Overall, NFCScaleAI provides a practical, efficient, and explainable framework for secure NFC transactions, IoT, and compliance-critical applications.

title: Explainability-Driven Autonomous AI Agents for Multi-Domain Applications
authors: John Krupavaram Pole, Reshma Thakkallapelly, Venkata Penumarthi, Ashraf Syed, Laxman Vattam, Komal Manohar Tekale
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The fast growth of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in a variety of fields has increased the need to have autonomous agents which can work reliably, transparently and ethically in the multi-domain environment. Although autonomous AI agents can be very versatile, their use cannot be explained or trusted, which is an obstacle to their widespread usage. This article introduces an explanatory-based framework of designing and implementing autonomous AI agents that are able to learn, adapt, and collaborate in fields like healthcare, finance, cybersecurity and smart manufacturing. It is a proposed model that combines interpretable machine learning models, causal reason mechanisms, and feedback in a human-in-the-loop to increase decision transparency without reducing autonomy. We propose an architecture that integrates symbolic reasoning with deep learning to maintain a tradeoff between interpretability and performance to allow agents to defend their actions instantly. A multi-domain assessment illustrates that explainability is able to elevate user trust and regulatory compliance along with boosting cross-domain generalization and endurance to adversarial environments. As indicated in experimental results, there is a significant change in interpretability measures and decision accuracy and adaptability in comparison with baseline black-box autonomous systems. The results indicate the promise of explainability as a foundation of the development of next-generation autonomous AI agents that will be responsible, scaleable, and human-compliant in their decision-making in key areas.

title: SLM-Fusion: A Unified Framework for Model Merging, Routing, and Multi-Model Orchestration
authors: Karun Thankachan
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The growing diversity of small and large language models (SLMs and LLMs) has led to the emergence of heterogeneous serving ecosystems, where optimal performance requires dynamic fusion, routing, and orchestration across multiple models. Existing approaches—such as model soups [1], mixture-of-experts architectures [2], and adaptive routing frameworks [3], [4]—address these challenges in isolation but lack a unified system that integrates parameter-space merging, decision-space routing, and cost-aware inference within a single composable architecture.We introduce slm-fusion, an open-source library that unifies model merging, routing, and serving under a modular, telemetry-driven framework. The system supports parameter-space fusion (ModelSoup, TIES-lite, LoRA merge), quantization-aware integration for int8/int4 models, and multi-level routing—including rule-based, learned, domain-specific, and cost-aware mechanisms. A FastAPI-based Fusion Gateway orchestrates speculative decoding, ensemble reranking, and mixture-of-agents flows, while telemetry and offline learning loops enable continual router optimization.The framework thus provides a comprehensive, practical path toward unified and transparent multi-model orchestration for language model inference.

title: Residual U-Net: An Enhanced Attention Mechanism for Segmentation of Retinal Lesions
authors: S. Vasuki, A. Maskura Fathima, B. Swetha, N.C. Thivyashree
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a high-risk condition affecting the eye, which progressively damages blood vessels, eventually causing irreversible vision loss in individuals with prolonged diabetes globally. Early detection of DR using advanced technologies is essential to prevent further vision deterioration. Segmenting retinal lesions is vital for understanding the nature of retinal damage and its treatment. In this work to achieve precise segmentation of retinal lesions, including hard exudates (EX), hemorrhages (HE), microaneurysms (MA), soft exudates (SE), and optic disk (OD), was carried out using a residual attention U-Net augmented with multi-scale attention blocks (MSAB) and lesion perception blocks (LPB) to incrementally refine the hierarchical and multi-scale features derived from fundus images. Furthermore, the OD was segmented to prevent misidentification as EX. To enhance the quality and contrast of the input images, preprocessing steps such as CLAHE-based contrast enhancement and normalization were utilized, and the masks were resized and one-hot encoded to ensure unbiased and consistent inputs. This study introduced a multi-channel tensor output, reduced by a 1×1 convolution layer followed by a sigmoid activation function, to obtain pixel-wise probabilities for each lesion type. The model achieved an accuracy of 99% on the IDRiD dataset, effectively segmenting various lesions and significantly enhancing sensitivity to faint and small lesions.

title: Explainable Deep Learning for Skin Disease Detection: From Black-Box to Trustworthy AI in Healthcare
authors: Muthamizhan.M, B.Sathyasri
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: In automatic classification of skin illnesses using thermoscopic and clinical photos, deep learning (DL) has shown remarkable performance, frequently outperforming dermatologists in accuracy. These models, sometimes known as "black-box" systems, are opaque, which raises serious questions about their acceptability, openness, and dependability in therapeutic settings. The goal of this research is to close the gap between reliable medical decision-making and high-performance AI by presenting an explainable deep learning (XDL) framework for skin disease identification. In order to visualize and interpret model predictions, the suggested framework combines post-hoc explainability techniques like Grad-CAM and LIME with convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for skin lesion categorization. Clinicians can verify AI outputs against established dermatological criteria by using the system to highlight discriminative picture regions that contribute to particular diagnosis. Benchmark data sets, such as HAM 10000 and ISIC2020, which cover several classifications like malignance, basal cell carcinoma, and benign keratosis, are used to assess the framework. The XDL model maintains interpretability using visual explanation maps while achieving over 92% classification accuracy, according to the results. Furthermore, we present a clinician feedback loop that allows for model improvement through user-in-the-loop learning, gradually increasing accuracy and confidence. Scalable integration into tele dermatology platforms is made possible by the system's optimization for deployment in cloud and edge environments. This method solves ethical and practical deployment issues while paving the way for transparent, clinician-friendly, and regulatory-compliant AI systems for dermatology.

title: Optimization of Energy Storage Capacity Allocation in Island Microgrids Considering Wind and PV Unit Operating Conditions
authors: Yuxuan Li, Shuai Su, Shiyu Chong, Xinxin Liu, Xueqing Chen, Zeyu Hao
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Microgrids with renewable energy systems represent an ideal power supply solution for remote islands. To address the challenge of configuring the capacity of energy storage systems in islanded microgrids, this paper proposes an optimization method that accounts for the operating conditions of wind and photovoltaic (PV) units. First, charge-discharge models of the energy storage system and output models of wind and solar power are established. Second, the wind turbine is modeled using a three-state operation model, while the PV unit is represented with a two-state operation model. Finally, a nonlinear inertial particle swarm optimization (NIPSO) algorithm is employed to minimize the total cost of the islanded microgrid configuration. A case study is presented to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: An Improved CEEMDAN-Based Signal Denoising Method for Wide-Band Oscillation
authors: Yanhui Xu, Ziyu Wang, Yangdou Liang, Yundan Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the integration of large-scale wind farms, wide-band oscillation events in power systems occur frequently, seriously threatening the security and stability of new power systems. Wide-band oscillation signals play a crucial role in further oscillation and suppression. However, the actual acquired signals are often contaminated by various noises, which interferes with the extraction of normal oscillation signal information. Therefore, accurately extracting the wide-band oscillation information embedded in the signals is particularly important. Existing pre-processing denoising methods rely heavily on data length, exhibit weak capability in handling complex signals, and are prone to causing modal aliasing. This paper employs the Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition with Adaptive Noise (CEEMDAN) method and calculates the sample entropy to filter out invalid components for pre-processing the measured signals. Subsequently, wavelet threshold filtering is applied to further denoise the pre-processed signals, obtaining more accurate denoised signals that preserve the original characteristics. Finally, simulations are carried out in the IEEE 39-node system based on MATLAB, and both Mean Square Error (RMSE) and Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) are introduced for comprehensive validation. The results demonstrate that the proposed method not only improves denoising performance but also enhances the ability to identify complex features within the signals.

title: Unit Clustering and Cluster-Based Dispatch Methodology for Long-Term Time-Sequential Production Simulation Considering UHVDC Outbound Transmission
authors: Jialu Li, Chenyao Pang, Qian Ma, Shuchen Kang, Huihong Luo, Meiying Huang, Zhenyuan Zhang, Yuechao Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Under the backdrop of increasing renewable energy penetration and the mismatch between regional power resources and load levels, the rational dispatch of electricity from energyrich areas to demand-intensive load centers has become a critical challenge in achieving the transformation of power grid energy structures. This paper takes the application scenario of renewable energy outbound transmission via Ultra-High voltage direct current (UHVDC) systems, analyzing the regulatory potential of tie-line transmission power. With the optimization objective of enhancing renewable energy utilization at the sending end, and incorporating unit clustering and cluster dispatch methods tailored for long-term chronological production simulation, a two-stage long-term production simulation method for outbound transmission power is developed. Finally, a comparative analysis conducted on the IEEE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex>-node test system verifies its optimization effectiveness for long-term production simulation.

title: PredictHealthCore: A Multi-Modal Framework for Disease Prediction Using Integrative Healthcare Data Analytics
authors: Rini Adiyattil, Aswathy Prakash G, S.Thangamayan, Thendral.T, Vidhi Agarwal, S.Isaiyazhini
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Disease prediction that is both accurate and prompt is essential for building better technologies in healthcare. Most models today focus on one kind of data such as EHR, medical images or sensor data alone which decreases the usefulness of their prediction outcomes. We have proposed PredictHealthCore which is a new deep learning framework that efficiently handles various healthcare data, for reliable disease prediction. For each type of input—images, texts and physiological records—it applies special feature extraction such as DenseNet, Transformer and TCNs, respectively. Using a hybrid way to learn fusion and using attention-weighted methods for encoding, this method becomes more efficient at crossing data domains. Compared to DALI, the predictive engine’s design includes CNN-LSTM, XGBoost and neurosymbolic reasoning to support high accuracy and explainable decisions. In ten different diseases, trials found that PredictHealthCore is 95.87% accurate, beating both traditional and deep learning methods. It also has good generalization capabilities, as seen by the AUC of 0.97 for cardiovascular disease and 0.98 for cancer detection. The findings confirm that unifying multiple healthcare methods in a single deep learning approach works well. Applying the proposed approach could be valuable in real hospitals, providing a big boost to precision medicine and intelligent healthcare analytics.

title: Optimizing Deployment of Unstructured Group Convolutions for Low Latency Inference
authors: Changxin Li, Sanmukh Kuppannagari
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Group convolutions are widely adopted in modern CNN architectures such as CondenseNet and ShuffleNet to enable efficient inference on GPUs. However, when the connection pattern between input and output channels does not exhibit regularity (unstructured group convolution), popular deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch) often struggle with load balancing and data reuse issues leading to reduced performance. In this paper, we present a comprehensive optimization framework that combines a Knapsack-based partitioning approach with Integer Linear Programming (ILP) and advanced matrix reordering to optimize the deployment of unstructured group convolutions which are used in popular models such as CondenseNets that learn group connections. Specifically, we use knapsack algorithm to determine partition (group) sizes for the connections to minimize execution time and use an Integer Linear Programming (ILP) to assign connections to the partitions (group) output by the knapsack algorithm. We also employ three matrix reordering strategies-Hierarchical Clustering (HC), Iterative Clustering (IC), and Reverse Cuthill-McKee (RCM) on the matrix representing the input-output connectivity pattern to further improve the performance of our scheduling algorithm. Our experiments on ShuffleNet and CondenseNet demonstrate up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.9 \times$</tex> speedups over PyTorch. Furthermore, augmenting ILP with reordering achieves an additional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.3 \times$</tex> improvement demonstrating the importance of optimizing for load balancing and data reuse.

title: A Low Voltage Ride-Through Control Strategy for Two-Stage Grid-Connected Photovoltaic Systems Under Asymmetrical Faults
authors: Yulei Cao, Jinbo Meng, Cunzhi Shan, Jing Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: During low voltage ride-through (LVRT), the twostage photovoltaic (PV) system may suffer from overcurrent in the inverter module and abnormal deviations in the DC-link voltage. To address these problems, this paper first proposes an adaptive voltage control strategy to stabilize the DC-link voltage. Additionally, when the system encounters asymmetrical grid faults, the Second-Order Generalized Integrator (SOGI) is utilized to decouple the positive- and negative-sequence components. Further, a novel current control strategy is developed, which ensures the system not only injects positive-sequence reactive current and absorbs negative-sequence reactive current, but also injects as much positive-sequence active power as possible into the grid. Meanwhile, a current limiting strategy is employed to ensure the inverter current stays within its safe limits. Finally, case studies are conducted to verify the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy.

title: Regional Coordinated Scheduling of Differentiated Electric Vehicle Charging Loads Based on Multi-Station Optimization
authors: Wei Jiang, Tiezheng Wang, Jinli Sun, Manjia Liu, Xuan Tong, Muchao Xiang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper proposes a differentiated Spatio-Temporal scheduling method for electric vehicle charging loads based on traffic-power dual-network coordination. A hierarchical road network model is established to analyze differentiated charging behaviors across slow, fast, and ultra-fast charging modes. A dayahead multi-scale matching mechanism is developed, where dayahead matching aggregates regional charging demands through virtual power plant mechanisms for temporal optimization. Case studies on the Sioux Falls network demonstrate that the proposed method achieves significant load volatility reduction (up to 26.6% standard deviation decrease), improved facility utilization, and multi-party benefits for users, operators, and the grid.

title: Research on Fault State Analysis and Fault-Tolerant Strategy for Dual Active Bridge Converters
authors: Yizhen Zhao, Hao Chang, Ying Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: To address the reduced operational capability of dual active bridge (DAB) converters caused by open-circuit faults, a fault-tolerant operation strategy named “Primary-Side Power Reduction with Secondary-Side Bypass Bridge Arm” (PPR-SBA) is proposed. First, based on the DAB converter topology, each fault mode under single-phase-shift control is analyzed, with detailed examination of the transient operational states and transmitted power for each fault. Second, corresponding fault-tolerant operation strategies are proposed based on distinct primary and secondary fault states. Finally, a DAB converter model is constructed using simulation software to validate the correctness and effectiveness of the proposed fault-tolerant strategy. The verification results demonstrate that this method can ensure a certain level of power transfer capability after an open-circuit fault, confirming its feasibility.

title: Fault Ride-Through Strategy of PV-Storage System with VSG Current Limiting Control
authors: Xiaoyan Lv, Chenxi Wu, Jian Chen, Kai Li, Ding Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy, grid-connected PV-storage systems face new challenges in ensuring stable operation during grid faults. Conventional photovoltaic (PV) converters under constant power control are unable to provide sufficient reactive support during voltage sags, while VSG-controlled energy storage converters can actively support the grid voltage but are prone to severe overcurrent issues. To address these problems, this paper proposes a fault ride-through strategy for grid-connected PV-storage systems based on VSG current-limiting control. The strategy integrates a PV voltage support control scheme, a virtual impedance technique, and a dynamic power regulation method to coordinate voltage support and current limitation. Electromagnetic transient simulations conducted on the MATLAB/Simulink platform verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. Results show that the strategy significantly enhances system fault tolerance by providing reactive support, stabilizing PCC voltage, and effectively suppressing VSG overcurrent, thereby improving the overall stability and reliability of PV -storage systems under fault conditions.

title: Research on the Error Characteristics of 500kV Capacitive Voltage Transformers Under Typical Insulation Faults
authors: Xiong Xiao, Mingchun Hou, Bingyuan Tan, Jixiang Liu, Lei Luo, Can Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In view of two typical insulation faults common in 500 kV CVT, the impact of the breakdown number and breakdown phase of high-voltage capacitors and low-voltage capacitors as well as the number and short-circuit phases of the primary and secondary sides of the intermediate transformer on the CVT error characteristics and output waveform was systematically studied. Research shows that when a CVT high-voltage capacitor breaks down, its ratio difference will be forward-biased and will increase as the number of capacitor breakdowns increases, but it will have little impact on the angular difference. When a low-voltage capacitor has breakdown, its ratio difference will be negatively biased, but the impact on the angular difference can be ignored. A short circuit in the primary winding of the intermediate transformer will cause the output voltage to rise and the ratio to be biased forward; while a short circuit between the secondary windings will cause the output voltage to drop and the ratio to be negatively biased; the angle difference of different interturn short circuit phases is also different and shows a certain degree of randomness.

title: Network Usage Charge Design for Peer-to-Peer Transactions Considering Network Constraints
authors: Quanxue Guan, Lihui Zheng, Xiaojun Tan, Yunjian Xu, Haoyong Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: As peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading becomes popular, its implementation without necessary safeguards poses a threat to the grid operation. Existing works fail to simultaneously coordinate conventional load supply and P2P trade on the same network, deliver dedicated signals to transactions that induce network violations, and establish a distributed and intuitive trading mechanism. To address the above problems, this paper proposes a two-stage framework based on a load priority rule. The first stage performs economic dispatch for conventional loads. The second stage employs a bi-layer network usage charge (NUC) algorithm for constrained prosumer trading. To achieve a targeted, fair, and stable NUC update, we design a novel weighted update rule in the distribution system operator (DSO) layer. An intuitive negotiation process is also designed to facilitate distributed P2P trading in the prosumer layer. Simulations show the proposed method narrows the voltage range from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.8-1.2$</tex> p.u. to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.95-1.05$</tex> p.u. and reduces the maximum absolute power deviation from 0.8 p.u. to 0.6 p.u.. Compared to the benchmark strategies, the proposed method also achieves higher social welfare.

title: Fixed-Frequency Model Predictive Control with Duty Cycle Optimization for Three-Level Gridconnected Converters
authors: Siyuan Wu, Jialei Wang, Hao Sun, Hao Li, Shaosen Li, Deqi Pu, Menglin Hu, Tao Ye
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Classical Finite Control Set Model Predictive Control (FCS-MPC) for three-level grid-connected inverters suffers from high computational burden, variable switching frequency, and difficulty in selecting weighting factors, limiting its application. To address these issues, this paper proposes a fixed-frequency FCS-MPC algorithm based on duty cycle optimization (DCO-MPC). The algorithm employs a sector partitioning method based on the deadbeat principle to select seven candidate real voltage vectors per control cycle. The optimal switching vector and its bound duty cycle optimization vector are then selected via cost function. These vectors are applied sequentially to the inverter with optimized dwell times. This approach achieves fixed switching frequency and significantly reduced grid current ripple while maintaining low computational complexity. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate substantial improvements over classical FCS-MPC, confirming the algorithm's practical value.

title: Analysis of Forced Convection Heat Transfer for Bipolar MVDC Power Cable Envisaged for Wide-Body All-Electric Aircraft
authors: Md Asifur Rahman, Mona Ghassemi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The future wide-body all-electric aircraft (AEA) requires low-mass, high-power, dense electric power systems (EPS) to deliver sufficient thrust power equivalent to that supplied by propulsion systems of conventional aircraft. To fulfill this requirement, the design of power cables is crucial since, due to their length, they occupy a substantial mass of the EPS and also ensure failsafe operation in harsh aviation environmental conditions. Thermal management of the cable is a major concern due to decreased heat transfer at lower air pressure at aviation altitudes compared to atmospheric pressure conditions at ground level. For this purpose, bipolar 5 kV Medium Voltage Direct Current (MVDC) cable for EPS architectures for a wide body AEA is proposed, designed, and fabricated with Micro-multilayer Multifunctional Electrical Insulation (MMEI) systems in our previous studies. Analysis of thermal performance of different shapes of the cable, such as cylindrical, cuboid, and coaxial configurations, is presented as a measure to increase radiative heat transfer to compensate for reduced convective heat transfer in our earlier work. Moreover, a performance analysis of several proposed MMEI designs is presented. In those studies, for convective heat transfer, only natural convection is considered, and for comparison, a parameter J is introduced, defined as the product of the total mass per unit length and the cross-sectional area of these cables. In this paper, the impact of forced convection heat transfer on bipolar MVDC cable designed for wide-body AEA is analyzed with a comparison to natural convection in terms of J.

title: DARENet: A Direction-Aware Road Extraction Method for Satellite Imagery
authors: Mingzhe Li, Zhilin Qu, Xulin Deng, Tianqi Wang, Zehua Chen
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Road extraction from remote sensing images (RSIs) has long been a critical task due to its broad application prospects such as digital twins for smart cities and intelligent transportation systems. However, extracting high-precision roads from RSIs remains challenging for the following reasons: (1) Ambiguity caused by background objects with geometric similarities to roads; (2) Topological discontinuities resulting from background occlusions. To address these issues, we propose a Direction-Aware Road Extraction network (DARENet). First, to address the ambiguity caused by insufficient structural features of extracted roads, we introduce a Multiscale Feature Extraction (MSFE) module with different directions in the skip connections; Second, to alleviate the issue of road topological discontinuities, we propose a Multidirectional Global-Local Fusion (MDGLF) module, which captures the relationship between roads and their environment through global and local processing. Finally, evaluations on three public datasets demonstrate that DARENet outperforms existing models. Our source code is available at https://github.com/ZehuaChenLab/DARENet.

title: Robustness-Guaranteed Reinforcement Learning under Uncertainties in Dynamics Modeling and State Estimates
authors: Duofeng Pan, Yongquan Huang, Rong Chen, Wenjie Lu, Manman Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Robust reinforcement learning enhances system robustness against uncertainties through bilevel optimization. However, the controller learned by reinforcement learning struggles to offer strict robustness guarantee, limiting its reliability in realworld applications. This paper presents robustness-guaranteed reinforcement learning framework under bounded uncertainties in dynamics modeling and state estimates. By describing the uncertainties and system dynamics models via ReLU neural networks, the states that most violate the proposed robustness conditions can be exactly found, and their robustness is prioritized during training. The solvability of the reinforcement learning problem is particularly studied, yielding a necessary condition that captures the adversarial relationship between control saturation and uncertainties. Subsequently, the attractive region can be pre-validated and the maximum robustness of the controller can be achieved by amplifying the uncertainties to a derived maximum extent in training. The proposed method has been fully evaluated in simulation and its advantages have been proven, including 3D quadrotor reference trajectory tracking tasks. The source code can be accessed via the GitHub repository at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/DuofengPan/RGRL_2025</uri>.

title: Diffusion Augmented Data for Inertial-Based Quadrotor Positioning
authors: Noa Cohen, Itzik Klein
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Inertial measurements play a vital role in motion tracking and navigation systems. In recent years, inertial sensing and state estimation pipelines have included deep learning algorithms to enhance their performance. The accuracy of such algorithms depends on the quality and diversity of the training dataset. Yet, the collection of large-scale inertial datasets remains a time-consuming and resource intensive process. This challenge has limited the advancement of robust inertial sensing learning models. Recently, diffusion models have emerged as a groundbreaking class of generative models that transform artificial data generation. They have demonstrated superior performance to adversarial generative networks and other state-of-the-art techniques in handling complex tasks. In this work, we propose a diffusion-driven framework for producing synthetic inertial data applicable to inertial-based quadrotor positioning. We introduce a novel three-stage pipeline that transforms inertial signals into image representations using delay embedding. It applies vision-based diffusion models conditioned on the target regression variables, and reconstructs synthetic time-series data. To show the effectiveness of our approach, we evaluated it on the EuRoC MAV dataset. Our comprehensive cross-validation evaluation across all 11 sequences of the EuRoC MAV dataset demonstrates that incorporating synthetic inertial data during training significantly improves regression performance, reducing the displacement error by 10.6% and the average position error by 31.1%. These results confirm that our diffusion-based generative model successfully captures the complex dynamics of inertial motion patterns, enabling the creation of diverse, realistic synthetic data that reduces dependence on extensive real-world data collection while providing high-quality training augmentation for neural inertial regression networks. Our method offers a scalable solution for data-constrained quadrotor applications by reducing costly real-world data collection.

title: A Low-Slow-Small UAV Radar target Recognition method Based on Point-Track feature Analysis and Multi-feature Fusion
authors: Jiawei Zhang, Zhenyu Luo, Yangguang Zhao, Meiguo Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) targets are characterized by low-altitude flight, small velocity, and small radar cross section (RCS), making them prone to being confused with small low-altitude flying targets such as birds in radar target recognition scenarios. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a radar target recognition method integrating kinematic characteristic classification and multi-feature fusion. The approach strategically partitions recognition targets into distinct category subspaces through kinematic analysis. We have put forward a novel “point-track-angle” (PTA) feature, which serves as an effective tool for clutter recognition. In the recognition of Low-Slow-Small (LSS) targets, we utilize different classifiers to recognize the PTA features, RCS, and micro-Doppler features, and then conduct further decision-level fusion of the classification results. Field experiments demonstrate significant performance improvement in distinguishing low-altitude small UAVs from avian targets. The recognition accuracy for both UAV and bird target points exceeds 92%, and the track recognition accuracy based on sequential statistics exceeds 95%. The target features utilized by this method are relatively easy to obtain, and it imposes low requirements on radar system architecture and resolution. It has been successfully deployed on the UAV surveillance radar developed by our laboratory.

title: DI-SESCINet: A Domain-Invariant Squeeze-and-Excitation SCI Network for Robotic Surgical Task Classification
authors: Fankai Sun, Nan Zheng, Haoxin Cui, Yujing Liang, Zhiqiang Yi, Yanfei Jia, Rong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Robotic surgery demands exceptional precision and stability, requiring surgeons to engage in continuous complex decision-making and coordination. This high cognitive load necessitates sustained concentration, accurate judgment, and strong psychological resilience, resulting in intricate neural activities within the surgeon’s brain. Consequently, investigating brain activity during various robotic surgical tasks is crucial for evaluating and enhancing surgical skills. However, effectively leveraging the spatial distribution of energy within the brain and the intrinsic connections between different brain regions presents significant challenges. This paper introduces a Domain-Invariant Squeeze-and-Excitation SCI Network (DI-SESCINet) designed for the classification of robotic surgical tasks. DI-SESCINet extracts and integrates spatial features derived from energy distribution and the intrinsic connections among different electroencephalogram (EEG) channels, effectively capturing the temporal patterns inherent in dynamic cognitive processes. Additionally, integrating a domain-invariant component aids in reducing sample-specific variability stemming from both individual differences and state changes, particularly in small sample sizes. Experimental results demonstrate that DI-SESCINet achieves superior classification performance, reaching 94.13%, underscoring its efficacy in enhancing the assessment of surgical skills.

title: Spatial-Frequency Feature Fusion: A Novel Deep Learning Architecture for Diabetic Wound Recovery
authors: Ahmed M. Gab Allah, Mohamed M.S. Gaballa, Dina M. Ibrahim, Nada M. Elshennawy
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The treatment of diabetic wounds presents a significant clinical obstacle. Traditional wound analysis methods are macroscopic and reliant on limited histological assessments. Biopsy sections stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) and Masson’s trichrome are examined visually. This paper presents a plan for evaluating wound healing in diabetic patients. The classification model integrates a DiscreteWavelet Feature Extraction (DWTF) module for spatial-frequency feature extraction and a Spatial Feature Extraction (SFE) module; moreover, it shares information between the two modules to improve feature representation to ensure nuclear morphological features. Additionally, a segmentation method is developed to evaluate wound dimensions and contours for precise automated monitoring. To assess the efficacy of our method, we conducted histochemical and histopathological evaluations of full-thickness excisional wounds, utilizing wound biopsies fromWistar rats subjected to two distinct histochemical treatments.We utilized the identical deep neural network (DNN) architecture for both training and analysis, attaining a mean test set accuracy of 93.17%. We reassessed our evaluation of chronic wound healing by analyzing enhancements that improved our model’s performance. This resulted in a mean Dice score of 93.84% for segmentation and classification accuracy exceeding 90%. These findings underscore the promise of combining histopathological imaging with artificial intelligence.

title: Cost Minimization in Energy Communities with Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning and Linear Programming
authors: Matic Pokorn, Andrej Čampa, Miha Smolnikar, Mihael Mohorčič, Jernej Hribar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With energy costs on the rise and with ever growing concern for environmental impact, energy providers and regulatory bodies have been pushing for dynamic energy prices as a means to encourage load shifting to reduce daily energy demand variance. Coupled with recent advancements in photovoltaics (PV) power generation and Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) technology, this has encouraged the development of energy communities with one of the goals to mitigate the effect of dynamic prices on homeowners’ energy bills without sacrificing comfort, while at the same time utilizing aggregation of Distributed Energy Resources (DER) to contribute to grid flexibility. In this paper, we present Mathematical Optimization and Deep Reinforcement learning for Energy Cost minimization (MODREC), a decentralised Community Energy Management System (CEMS). MODREC leverages Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (MADRL) coupled with Linear Programming (LP) to minimize cost in an energy community by intelligently charging and discharging household BESSs while assuming non-elastic consumer loads. MODREC follows an LP-guided training pipeline, where an optimal strategy computed with LP on historical data is employed to train a set of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) agents, each assigned to a household in the community, that minimize a common cost function. Our main contribution lies in the system-level integration of LP-derived expert supervision with decentralized multi-agent control for community energy cost minimization under dynamic pricing. With MODREC, we manage to save up to 30% of energy costs compared to conventional approaches and efficiently shift energy load to off-peak hours.

title: Dynamic Resistance Investigation of HTS Coated Conductors Carrying a Direct Current Exposed to Pulsed Magnetic Field
authors: Abdelillah Aougbi, Yazid Statra, Yahia Achour, Hocine Menana, Khalil Touimi, Achour Ales, Abdelhalim Zaoui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: High temperature superconducting (HTS) flux pumps have emerged as a promising contactless method for magnetizing closed HTS magnets. These systems rely on HTS switches, which operate through the principle of dynamic resistance, which is a phenomenon where a voltage is induced along a type II HTS tape carrying a direct current (DC) when exposed to a changing magnetic field. While this effect is well known, conventional methods using alternating current (AC) sinusoidal magnetic fields often require demanding conditions, such as high amplitudes and frequencies of applied field to attain high values of dynamic resistance. In this work, we explore a novel approach to generating dynamic voltage in HTS tapes carrying DC transport current by employing pulsed magnetic fields (PMF). To investigate this approach, an experimental bench was designed and implemented to measure the dynamic voltage under these conditions. Additionally, a two-dimensional (2D) electromagnetic thermal coupled model, based on the finite element method, was used to check out the measured quantities.

title: RL-Augmented MPC for Non-Gaited Legged and Hybrid Locomotion_supp1-3675839.mp4
authors: Andrea Patrizi
publish: N/A
abstract: We propose a contact-explicit hierarchical architecture coupling Reinforcement Learning (RL) and Model Predictive Control (MPC), where a high-level RL agent provides gait and navigation commands to a low-level locomotion MPC. This off loads the combinatorial burden of contact timing from the MPC by learning acyclic gaits through trial and error in simulation. We show that only a minimal set of rewards and limited tuning are required to obtain effective policies. We validate the architecture in simulation across robotic platforms spanning 50kg to 120kg and different MPC implementations, observing the emergence of acyclic gaits and timing adaptations in flat-terrain legged and hybrid locomotion, and further demonstrating extensibility to non-flat terrains. Across all platforms, we achieve zero-shot sim to-sim transfer without domain randomization, and we further demonstrate zero-shot sim-to-real transfer without domain ran domization on Centauro, our 120kg wheeled-legged humanoid robot. We make our software framework and evaluation results publicly available at https://github.com/AndrePatri/AugMPC.

title: Smart Radiology System: Pneumonia Identification through a VGG16-Enhanced CNN Model
authors: Sivanesh K, Rajkumar K, Shajilin Loret J B
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Respiratory diseases, especially pneumonia, are some of the most significant health issues affecting microscopic air spaces (alveoli) in lung tissues, most commonly due to infection with pathogenic bacteria. Once there is an infectious process, these microscopic air spaces fill with inflammatory fluid and/or pus, which can greatly affect the ability to breathe. The current medical diagnosis, especially of pneumonia, is heavily dependent on diagnostic imaging through radiological investigations, including chest radiographs (imaging), ultrasound (imaging), and histopathological evaluations of objective findings, in conjunction with clinical evaluations. Lack of diagnostic confidence during this time can lead to delays in treatment, mismanagement clinically, and/or adverse patient outcomes. Exciting advances in computational intelligence, particularly utilizing machine learning approaches, have transformed the way we interpret medical diagnostic procedures, leading to models that could automate these processes for timely and accurate identification of pulmonary conditions. Utilizing chest X-ray scans, this study provides a reliable convolutional neural network (CNN) approach to helping distinguish between healthy and pneumonia-affected individuals. The experimental framework utilized a dataset consisting of approximately 20,000 chest X-ray images, preprocessed to a resolution of 224 × 224 pixels and a batch size of 32 during training. The model demonstrated an impressive near 95 percent accuracy classification, demonstrating its capability in medical imaging recognition. In addition to detecting pneumonia in a general sense, the model was also capable in distinguishing between bacterial infections, viral infection, and COVID-19 presentations based solely on X-ray imagery.

title: CALL: Context-Aware Low-Latency Retrieval in Disk-Based Vector Databases
authors: Yeonwoo Jeong, Hyunji Cho, Kyuli Park, Youngjae Kim, Sungyong Park
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Embedding models capture both semantic and syntactic structures of queries, often mapping different queries to similar regions in vector space. This results in nonuniform cluster access patterns in modern disk-based vector databases. While existing approaches optimize individual queries, they overlook the impact of cluster access patterns, failing to account for the locality effects of queries that access similar clusters. This oversight increases cache miss penalty. To minimize the cache miss penalty, we propose CALL, a context-aware query grouping mechanism that organizes queries based on shared cluster access patterns. Additionally, CALL incorporates a group-aware prefetching method to minimize cache misses during transitions between query groups and latency-aware cluster loading. Experimental results show that CALL reduces the 99th percentile tail latency by up to 33 % while consistently maintaining a higher cache hit ratio, substantially reducing search latency.

title: ZeroRush: Iot-Based Motorbike Monitoring with Helmet Detection and Eco-Safe Driving Score Using Adaptive Penalty Scoring Method
authors: Bhakti Kamate, Gunapriya B, Nandana Raj, Sania Das, Sudeep Kumar Dutta, Shivam Pandey
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: ZeroRush is an intelligent driving assistance system aimed at improving road safety and promoting eco-friendly driving in urban environments. It integrates GPS, accelerometer sensors, and a helmet detection module to monitor riding behavior and safety compliance in real time. Unsafe actions such as overspeeding, harsh acceleration, or riding without a helmet are detected instantly, and the system provides immediate feedback while dynamically updating a novel eco-score algorithm that reflects both driving efficiency and rider safety. Built on a low-cost microcontroller platform, ZeroRush is compact, energy-efficient, and designed for seamless integration into two-wheelers. By combining real-time eco-driving support with helmet compliance monitoring, the system contributes directly to Sustainable Development Goal 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities, offering measurable potential to reduce accidents, fuel consumption, and environmental impact

title: Malaria Detection with ML classifiers and Custom CNN using LBP Feature Extraction
authors: Dhananjay Pratap Singh Chandel, Sandeep Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Malaria is one of the most common infectious disease worldwide and in India, caused by plasmodium parasite and spread by female mosquitoes. Though being diagnosable through traditional techniques like examining blood smear image under microscope, the process is pretty much time consuming and labour intensive.In the modern era when computer vision, ML&DL have advanced a lot, several attempts and methodologies have been implemented to solve this task of malaria detection. In the study conducted in this paper we have developed methods to detect malaria parasite on small and large datasets of malaria infected and uninfected blood smear images. We developed SVM, KNN and CNN models with rigorous pre-processing and LBP feature extraction applied to these images. We have achieved 99.25% on small dataset with SVM rbf kernel. On the large dataset, applying preprocessing with Otsu thresholding, contour masking and CLAHE, followed LBP Feature Extraction data fed into training custom CNN model led to achieving 99.45% accuracy on unseen test data. This Highlights the significant achivement in accuracy for malaria detection task.

title: A GAN-Based Data Augmentation Method for Imbalanced Multi-Class Skin Lesion Classification
authors: S. Devansh Raj, N. Bhuvan, G. Aravind, B. Lakshmi Prasanna
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Class imbalance in dermatoscopic image datasets such as HAM10000 poses a critical challenge for automated skin lesion classification, often resulting in poor recognition of rare but clinically significant lesion categories. This study introduces a practical three-phase pipeline: (i) Baseline—EfficientNet-B3 trained on the original imbalanced dataset, (ii) Traditional Augmentation—a balanced dataset created through geometric and photometric transformations, and (iii) Hybrid Augmentation—traditional augmentation combined with a limited number of class-conditional GAN (cDCGAN) synthetic images.The results show substantial improvements across all phases. Traditional augmentation improved overall accuracy from 79% to 98%, and the hybrid GAN-augmented strategy further increased macro-F1 to 0.985, with notable recall gains for melanoma, basal cell carcinoma, and dermatofibroma. These improvements highlight that even a modest number of high-quality synthetic images (500 per class) can significantly boost minority-class performance without the computational burden of large-scale GAN balancing.This work provides a computationally efficient and clinically relevant framework for robust skin lesion classification. The findings emphasize the potential of hybrid augmentation strategies to improve diagnostic support systems, particularly for underrepresented lesion categories critical to early cancer detection.

title: Scalable XML Parsing and XPath Querying with Regular Expression Support
authors: Robert Samuel, Rupesh Nasre
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Data sizes are growing rapidly, leading to large document sizes and higher processing times. With the unprecedented growth in structured and semi-structured data, and their processing in various applications, XML continues to be a preferred choice as a standard data format and exchange. This standardization permits vendors to build tools for parsing and querying XML data. Unfortunately, the absolute running times of these tools quickly become prohibitive for large XML data. In such a situation, parallel processing becomes imperative. However, parsing and querying XML documents in parallel present significant challenges. Parsing is particularly difficult due to the inherent sequential and hierarchical nature of XML data, while complex querying with regular expressions is constrained by memory limitations. In this work, we develop a parallel XML parser and a parallel XML query engine with support for regular expressions. It supports not only the traditional startswith and endswith XPath queries, but also presence of arbitrary Kleene-closures. An extensive experimental evaluation of our tool illustrates significant improvements over standard libXML, especially at scale, achieving over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$32 \times$</tex> speedup.

title: Lyapunov-Based Feasibility Recovery for Carbon-Aware Optimal Power Flow
authors: Musen Lin, Weiye Zheng
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The inherent computational characteristics of carbon emission flow introduce non-convex constraints, which cause challenges in the solution process of the optimal power flow problem, leading to computational difficulties and prolonged solution times. Furthermore, solving these non-convex constraints via relaxation methods can result in infeasible solutions. To address this, this paper proposes a novel feasible solution recovery method designed to resolve the infeasibility issues encountered in the carbon-aware optimal power flow model. The method is based on Lyapunov theory, which utilizes an energy function to construct an artificial dynamic system ensuring asymptotic stability. As the variables in the dynamic system evolve along a predetermined trajectory, the Lyapunov condition is satisfied, thus guaranteeing the asymptotic convergence of the equilibrium point of the system. Numerical results demonstrate that, compared to the original infeasible solutions, the proposed recovery method significantly improves the degree of constraint violation and always ensures convergence to the nearest feasible solution.

title: A Robust Hierarchical Control Strategy for Microgrids Considering Controller Soft Faults
authors: Zhida Huang, Wei Zhang, Jingjing Li, Shujin Chen, Shengjie Xiao, Hua Han, Mei Su
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Islanded AC microgrids (IACMGs) is the optimal solution for power supply in areas inaccessible to transmission infrastructure. It is known that the normal operation of its controllers in IACMGs is the key to ensuring system power balance and voltage and frequency stability. However, controller software faults occur occasionally, which will deteriorate system performance and threaten normal system operation. To address these issues, a robust hierarchical cooperative control strategy based on universal droop control, distributed fault and state estimation, and an adaptive fault-tolerant mechanism is proposed for IACMGs with arbitrary lines impedance. This approach achieves precise reactive power sharing, zero-error bus voltage regulation, and frequency support even when controller soft faults occur, thereby improving the stability, reliability, and robustness of IACMGs. Both theoretical analysis and experimental results validate the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed hierarchical control framework.

title: Electric Field Sensor with High Sensitivity via Dielectric Self-Assembly Enhancement
authors: Yao Zheng, Fusheng Zhou, Chao Gao, Le Yan, Zikui Shen, Yibo Yang, Yanpeng Hao
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This study addresses the limitations of conventional electric field sensors (low sensitivity, poor antiinterference) by enhancing the dielectric constant of the intermediate layer. A high-k(16) PDMS composite was fabricated via electric-field-induced directional self-assembly of 1D TiO2 fillers in silicone rubber, showing superior performance over random samples <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(k=6)$</tex> and pure PDMS <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{k}=3.3)$</tex> with insulation stability (imaginary part <x0.03). Integrated with a PZT-5H piezoelectric layer operating in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{d}_{31}$</tex> mode, the sensor couples electric field-induced strain to capacitance changes. Simulations revealed a 400% sensitivity boost under nonlinear dielectric conditions compared to the constant- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\kappa$</tex> model. Experiments confirmed nonlinear capacitance behavior in the composite (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{C}=0.21$</tex> at small strains, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.5 \times$</tex> random samples), validating enhanced weak-field detection. This material-structure co-optimization achieves high precision without complicating device design, offering a new pathway for sensitive electric field sensing in power grid.

title: Electro-Thermal Coupling Calculation in Submarine Cables: A Method Based on Fiber-Optic Temperature Monitoring and Analysis of its Accuracy Determinants
authors: Ji Wu, Hongyi Zhong, Xin Yu, Yanpeng Hao, Shihu Yu, Yashuang Zheng
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Monitoring the conductor temperature constitutes a crucial index for assessing the operational health of 500 kV oil-filled submarine cables; however, obtaining direct insitu measurements remains a formidable technical hurdle. Traditional thermal field simulations predominantly derive conductor temperature from ambient environmental conditions, a method that frequently introduces substantial errors relative to actual values. To address this limitation, this paper constructs an electro-thermal coupling model specifically for 500 kV oilfilled cables, treating surface temperature data acquired from ampacity experiments as the definitive boundary condition. This study further investigates the key determinants influencing the core temperature computation within this framework. Analysis confirms that integrating measured surface temperature as the boundary condition in electro-thermal calculations drastically mitigates estimation errors. This finding substantiates the engineering feasibility of applying fiber-optic sensing to deduce conductor temperature. Moreover, sensitivity analysis reveals that the thermal conductivity of impregnated paper tapes and the dielectric loss tangent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\tan \delta)$</tex> of the insulation are the dominant factors affecting the results, whereas the thermal properties of other structural layers play a subordinate role. Consequently, this research delivers the necessary theoretical framework and computational verification for utilizing fiberoptic technology in submarine cable status monitoring.

title: Day-ahead Real-time Optimal Operation Method for Distribution Grid Based on Supply-Demand Bilateral Cooperative Dispatch
authors: Zhanpeng Liu, Shuai Fan, Li Li, Jing Wang, Renke Huang, Guangyu He
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the accelerated construction of new power system, distributed photovoltaic and electric vehicles and other new loads continue to access the grid, resulting in the existing part of the distribution network hosting capacity is insufficient, causing risk of voltage overruns, distribution area transformers overload, etc., how to carry out collaborative dynamic dispatching of the supply and demand sides of the resources in the actual operation process in order to achieve the economic and safe operation of the distribution network remains to be studied. In this paper, we propose a day-ahead and real-time operation method for distribution networks based on supply and demand side cooperative dispatching. In day-ahead stage, the typical scenarios of source-load are predicted by historical data, and the distributed algorithm is used to obtain the dayahead planned power of supply-side soft open point and demand-side flexible resources with the goal of minimizing the operation cost; in the intra-day stage, the impacts of the sourceload uncertainties are corrected by the real-time rolling optimization in order to ensure the safe and economic operation of the distribution network and better host subordinate sourceload.

title: An Energy Management Framework for VPP based on a Dynamic Price Incentive Algorithm
authors: Weiwei Chen, Haipeng Cai, Jing Wang, Zhenshang Wang, Zhenshang Wang, Yuming Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Virtual Power Plants (VPPs) have emerged as a promising solution for integrating Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) into modern distribution networks. However, smallcapacity DERs face inherent limitations, including constrained communication capability, heterogeneous operational requirements, and time-varying output fluctuations, which significantly complicate VPP energy management. To mitigate these challenges, this paper proposes a dynamic price incentive-based energy management framework. The framework explicitly accounts for short-term load fluctuations and ensures DER prosumers' economic benefits through intraday average price constraints. For practical online implementation, we develop a Lyapunov optimization-based algorithm to solve the real-time optimal control problem. Case studies demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework in terms of both deviation control and economic efficiency.
